content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Jeff Walter 
City Attorney 
City of Martinez 
670 W. Napa Street, Suite F 
Sonoma, CA 95476 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-155 
Dear Mr. Walter: 
October 11, 2018 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the Mayor of the City of 
Martinez, Rob Schroder, regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act 
(the "Act") 1 and Section 1090. Please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In 
re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete 
and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you 
should contact us for additional advice. 
In regard to our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all 
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Contra Costa County 
District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097. l(c)(3).) We did not receive a 
written response from either entity. (Section 1097.1 ( c )( 4 ). ) We are also required to advise you that, 
for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding 
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.l(c)(5).) 
QUESTION 
Under the Act and Section 1090, may Mayor Schroder take part in City Council decisions 
related to the lease of a City-owned property to a local Boys & Girls Club, given that Mayor 
Schroder is a member of the Boys & Girls Club's advisory board? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. Mayor Schroder may take part in the City Council's decisions to approve a lease of a 
City-owned property to the local Boys & Girls Club because he does not have a disqualifying 
conflict of interest in the decisions under the Act. Additionally, under Section 1091.5(a)(8), Mayor 
Schroder has a noninterest in a lease between the City and the Boys & Girls Club. Therefore, 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-18-155 
Page No. 2 
Section 1090 does not preclude Mayor Schroder or the City Council from making or participating in 
a lease with the Boys & Girls Club. 
FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
Mayor Schroder is a member of the Martinez City Council. Mayor Schroder is also a 
member of the advisory committee for the local Boys & Girls Club, a non-profit organization. The 
Boys & Girls Club provides social, recreational, educational, and professional support services to 
young people. 2 
Mayor Schroder is not a member of the Board of Directors for the Boys & Girls Club. He 
has no authority to bind or commit the Boys & Girls Club or its Board of Directors to any action. 
He is not employed by the Boys & Girls Club and receives no compensation whatsoever for his 
position on the advisory board. 
The Boys & Girls Club is currently in negotiations with the City to lease a City-owned 
property.3 
ANALYSIS 
The Political Reform Act 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or 
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official has a financial interest. Pertinent to your facts, the Act's conflict of interest provisions apply 
to financial interests based on the following: 
• 
An interest in a business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment of 
$2,000 or more (Section 87103(a)); or in which the official is a director, officer, partner, 
trustee, employee, or holds any position of management. (Section 87103(d).) 
• 
An interest in a source of income to the official, including promised income, which 
aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(c).) 
• 
The official's interest in his or her personal finances and those of immediate family 
members. (Section 87103.) 
In this case, Mayor Schroder receives no income whatsoever for his position as an 
uncompensated officer with the Boys & Girls Club's advisory board. You have not provided any 
facts indicating that the Boys & Girls Club is a source of income to Mayor Schroder. Additionally, 
as a nonprofit organization, the Boys & Girls Club is not a "business entity" as defined by the Act. 
(Section 82005.) Further, as Mayor Schroder does not receive any compensation whatsoever for his 
2 See https://bgccontracosta.org/ 
3 You have not provided facts to indicate that the City Council, or any Council members, participated in any of 
the negotiations to date related to the City-owned property. 


File No. A-18-155 
Page No. 3 
advisory position with the Boys & Girls Club, none of the provided facts suggest the decision to 
approve a lease of a City-owned property to the Boys & Girls Club will have any financial effect on 
his or his immediate family's personal finances. Therefore, based on the facts you have provided, 
Mayor Schroder does not have a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act in the City 
Council's decisions to lease a City-owned property to the Boys & Girls Club. 
Section 1090 
Generally, Section 1090 prohibits public officers, while acting in their official capacities, 
from making contracts in which they are financially interested. The prohibition applies regardless of 
whether the terms of the contract are fair and equitable to all parties. (Thomson v. Call (1985) 38 
Cal.3d 633, 646.) Section 1090 is concerned with financial interests, other than remote interests or 
noninterests, that prevent officials from exercising absolute loyalty and undivided allegiance in 
furthering the best interests of their agencies. (Stigall v. Taft (1962) 58 Cal.2d 565, 569.) An officer 
is conclusively presumed to be involved in the making of his or her agency's contracts when the 
officer is a member of a board or commission that has the power to execute the contract at issue. 
(Thomson, supra, p. 649.) As a general rule, when Section 1090 is applicable to one member of a 
governing body of a public entity, the entire governing body is precluded from entering into the 
contract. (Stigall, supra, at p. 569.) 
The term "financially interested" contained in Section 1090 has been defined as follows: 
The phrase 'financially interested' as used in Government Code 
section I 090 means any financial interest which might interfere with a city officer's 
unqualified devotion to his public duty. The interest may be direct or indirect. It 
includes any monetary or proprietary benefit, or gain of any sort, or the contingent 
possibility of monetary or proprietary benefits. The interest is direct when the city 
officer, in his official capacity, does business with himself in his private capacity. 
The interest is indirect when the city officer, or the board of which he is a member, 
enters into a contract in his or its official capacity with an individual or business 
firm, which individual or business firm, by reason of the city officer's relationship to 
the individual or business firm at the time the contract is entered into, is in a position 
to render actual or potential pecuniary benefits directly or indirectly to the city 
officer based on the contract the individual or business firm has received. 
(88 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 32, 36.) 
Nonetheless, Section 1091.5(a) establishes that an officer is not interested in a contract if his 
or her interest is: 
(8) That of a noncompensated officer4 of a nonprofit, tax-exempt 
corporation, which, as one of its primary purposes, supports the functions of the 
4 For purposes of this exception, an officer is "noncompensated" even though he or she receives 
reimbursement from the nonprofit, tax-exempt corporation for necessary travel and other actual expenses incurred in 
performing the duties of his or her office. 


File No. A-18-155 
Page No. 4 
body or board or to which the body or board has a legal obligation to give particular 
consideration, and provided further that this interest is noted in its official records. 5 
A primary purpose of the Boys & Girls Club is to provide social, recreational, and 
educational support for young people. Such a purpose supports the functions of the City by 
promoting a variety of programs for the City's youth population. Therefore, as an uncompensated 
officer of a nonprofit, tax-exempt corporation, Section 1090 does not prohibit Mayor Schroder from 
participating in City Council decisions concerning the lease of City-owned property to the Boys & 
Girls Club. Moreover, because Mayor Schroder does not have a financial interest in contracts 
between the City and the Boys.& Girls Club under Section 1091.5(a)(8), Section 1090 does not 
preclude the City Council from entering into a contract with the Boys & Girls Club. However, 
should Mayor Schroder participate in a contract involving the Boys & Girls Club, he must disclose 
his interest in the City Council's official records. 
Accordingly, for purposes of the Act, Mayor Schroder does not have a disqualifying conflict 
of interest in City Council decisions between the City and the Boys & Girls Club for the lease of a 
City-owned property. Additionally, because Mayor Schroder is not interested in a contract between 
the City and the Boys & Girls Club pursuant to Section 1091.5(a)(8), Section 1090 does not 
prohibit the City Council from entering into a lease with the Boys & Girls Club. So long as Mayor · 
Schroder notes his interest in the Boys & Girls Club in the City Council's official records, he may 
participate in the making of the lease in his official capacity. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
By: 
SKP:jgl 
5 We acknowledge that Mayor Schroder' s position on the advisory board is not identical to that of an "officer" 
on a corporation's board of directors. (See 65 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 41, 56 (1982) [applying the term "officer" to a 
member of a board of directors for a nonprofit corporation].) However, because his interest is further removed than the 
interest of an actual board member to a nonprofit corporation, this exception should apply equally to Mayor Schroder.

related to the lease of a City-owned property to a local Boys & Girls Club, given that Mayor 
Schroder is a member of the Boys & Girls Club's advisory board?

Schroder has a noninterest in a lease between the City and the Boys & Girls Club. Therefore,Section 1090 does not preclude Mayor Schroder or the City Council from making or participating in 
a lease with the Boys & Girls Club.
